title: Healey calls on aviation company operating at Hanscom to cut ties with ICE
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Gov. Maura Healey urges Signature Aviation to cut ties with ICE at Hanscom Field, citing concerns about deportation practices.
Signature Aviation's cooperation supports 132 ICE deportation flights from Hanscom Field during the first year of Trump's term, according to data.
Healey criticizes ICE's budget and accuses the agency of violating rights and targeting non-criminal community members in Massachusetts.

title: Delivery person falls through a shaft outside Boston’s Omni Parker House
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: A delivery worker fell 20 feet through a metal grate behind Boston's Omni Parker House, sustaining injuries.
The Boston Fire Department rescued the worker, and he was transported to a hospital for treatment.
Boston Inspectional Services cited the hotel for building code violations due to rotted brackets beneath the grate.

title: Quincy gas station looking for violist to ‘serenade’ customers
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Quincy Gas owner Harvey Kertzman seeks a violist to serenade customers, posting signs at gas pumps.
A retired violist has been found, and performances will start with warmer weather in Quincy, Massachusetts.
Quincy Gas features unique offerings like penny candy, inspirational quotes, and complimentary bottled water.

title: Report: Patriots agree to sign edge rusher Dre’Mont Jones
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots sign edge rusher Dre'Mont Jones for up to $39.5 million over three years, per NFL Media reports.
Jones had 7 sacks and 43 tackles last season, playing for the Ravens after a mid-season trade from Tennessee.
Patriots' pass-rush struggled in 2025, ranking 26th in both sacks and pressures, prompting the Jones acquisition.

title: How a Boston Marathon runner finds purpose in mentoring and miles
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Victoria Walker will run the 2026 Boston Marathon supporting Minds Matter Boston for high-achieving, low-income students.
Walker mentors two 16-year-olds through Minds Matter Boston, helping them with college applications and personal growth.
Running has provided Walker with community and purpose, blending her passions for mentoring and long-distance running.

title: Meet the New England Aquarium’s viral seal and his rubber duckie pal
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Reggae, a harbor seal at the New England Aquarium, went viral for playing with a rubber duckie in a video.
The video became the aquarium's second-best performing Instagram post with nearly 500,000 views and 100,000 engagements.
Trainers use cognitive enrichment activities with the seals, including Reggae, to simulate natural behaviors found in the wild.

title: What we know about Bravo’s ‘The Real Housewives of Rhode Island’
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: 'The Real Housewives of Rhode Island' premieres April 2 on Bravo, marking the first New England-based edition of the franchise.
The cast includes former local TV anchor Rosie DiMare and 'Cannabis Queen' Liz McGraw, highlighting Rhode Island's Italian community.
The Red Sox will host a Bravo Night at Fenway Park on April 17 with the show’s cast attending a game against the Detroit Tigers.

title: 24 Boston businesses approved for new or upgraded liquor licenses
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Boston approved liquor licenses for 24 businesses, including new and upgraded licenses, under recent state legislation.
Licenses prioritize less affluent areas like East Boston and Roslindale, easing access and reducing costs for restaurants.
Businesses like Pizzeria Regina and Rocco's Cucina upgraded to all-alcohol licenses as part of recent legislative efforts.

title: Mass. man nabbed after allegedly driving over 100 mph in N.H.
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A 21-year-old Attleboro man was arrested for driving 104 mph in a 55 mph zone on Route 202, Rindge.
The incident occurred late Wednesday near Sears Drive, involving a 2020 Kia Stinger, according to Rindge Police.
The driver faces charges of reckless operation, with a court date set for April 5 and potential fines and suspension.

title: AirTag leads volunteers to hidden cat and six kittens in Waltham
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: The Cat Connection used an AirTag to locate a stray cat and her six kittens at a Waltham storage site.
Volunteers rescued the cat and kittens during a New England blizzard and moved them to a foster home.
Emerald, the stray cat, and her kittens will be available for adoption in about two months.
